title: Lovely Molten Moroccan Chinese Long Beans Zaalouk
description: A plant-based Moroccan dish featuring roasted Chinese long beans in a rich and spicy glaze, perfect for adventurous eaters. This recipe is a unique fusion of Moroccan and Chinese flavors.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, toss the Chinese long beans with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the beans out in a single layer on a baking sheet and roast for 20 minutes, or until they are tender and lightly browned.
In a large skillet, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the skillet and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Stir in the tomato paste, smoked paprika, and cumin, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the roasted Chinese long beans to the skillet and stir to combine with the onion and spice mixture.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the beans are well coated with the glaze.
